methods.method_name: Isolation of Epiphycan from Bovine Fetal Epiphyseal Cartilage
methods.description: Isolation of dermatan sulfate proteoglycans from bovine fetal epiphyseal cartilage using ion-exchange and gel permeation chromatography.

steps.description: Dermatan sulfate proteoglycans were isolated from bovine fetal epiphyseal cartilage by ion-exchange followed by gel permeation chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate dermatan sulfate proteoglycans using ion-exchange and gel permeation chromatography

methods.description: Chromatographic separation of proteoglycan-containing fractions using an octyl-Sepharose column.

steps.description: The DEAE-bound and eluted proteoglycan-containing fractions were applied to an octyl-Sepharose column equilibrated with 2 M GdnHCl and 0.15 M sodium acetate, pH 6.3, at 25°C and allowed to bind for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: apply proteoglycan fractions to octyl-Sepharose column for binding

steps.description: The column was washed with 3 volumes of 2 M GdnHCl, and the proteoglycans were eluted with a linear gradient of 2-6 M GdnHCl.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and elute proteoglycans from octyl-Sepharose column

steps.description: Fractions were analyzed for uronate, revealing the presence of two major peaks.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fractions for uronate content

methods.description: Separation of proteoglycans using Zn2+ chelate chromatography to further purify epiphycan and decorin.

steps.description: Chelating Sepharose was charged with ZnCl2 and washed with water, then packed into a column and equilibrated with Chelex 100-treated buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and equilibrate Zn2+ chelate column

steps.description: The concentrated and dialyzed eluate from the octyl-Sepharose column was applied to the Zn2+ chelate column and allowed to bind for 2 h at 25°C.
steps.searchable_summary: apply proteoglycan eluate to Zn2+ chelate column

steps.description: The column was eluted with NaCl and Tris-HCl, followed by a linear pH gradient. Fractions were collected and monitored for uronate, protein, and pH.
steps.searchable_summary: elute and monitor fractions from Zn2+ chelate column

methods.description: Sequencing of protein and peptide samples using an automated sequencer and HPLC.

steps.description: Protein and peptide samples were sequenced using an Applied Biosystems 477A sequencer with on-line detection of phenylthiohydantoin-derivatives on a 120A microbore HPLC.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence proteins and peptides using automated sequencer and HPLC

methods.description: Mapping of peptides using trypsin digestion and chromatography techniques to identify unique peptides.

steps.description: Initial peptide mapping was performed using trypsin and a mixture of epiphycan and decorin as the substrate.
steps.searchable_summary: perform trypsin digestion for peptide mapping

steps.description: Peptides unique to the map of epiphycan-containing material were sequenced.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence unique peptides from epiphycan map

steps.description: Peptides were separated by gel permeation chromatography on a Superose 12 column, followed by reversed-phase HPLC of individual fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: separate peptides using chromatography and HPLC

methods.method_name: Identification of the Epiphycan-coding cDNA Sequence
methods.description: Design and use of degenerate oligonucleotide primers for PCR amplification and sequencing of the epiphycan-coding cDNA sequence.

steps.description: Initial protein sequence data enabled the design of two degenerate oligonucleotide primers expected to give a product of 230 base pairs.
steps.searchable_summary: design degenerate primers for PCR

steps.description: Reverse transcription-PCR of bovine chondrocyte RNA, with an annealing temperature of 40°C, gave a band of the expected size, which was excised from an agarose gel.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RT-PCR and gel excision

steps.description: The PCR product was reamplified using similar primers modified with EcoRI and BamHI clamps and cloned into pBluescript™.
steps.searchable_summary: reamplify and clone PCR product

steps.description: Sequence analysis of the cloned insert indicated that the PCR product corresponded to the determined protein sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sequence of cloned insert

methods.method_name: Iodination and Isolation of Radiolabeled Proteoglycans
methods.description: Radiolabeling of proteoglycans using the chloramine-T method and subsequent isolation using chromatography.

steps.description: The SLRPs epiphycan, decorin, and biglycan were radiolabeled with [125I]iodine by the chloramine-T method.
steps.searchable_summary: radiolabel proteoglycans using chloramine-T method

steps.description: The labeled proteoglycan was separated from unincorporated Na125I on a PD-10 column, followed by a mono-Q-Sepharose column.
steps.searchable_summary: separate labeled proteoglycan using chromatography

methods.description: Estimation of molecular masses of proteoglycans using gel permeation chromatography.

steps.description: Labeled proteoglycans were further purified on a DEAE-Sepharose column. Molecular masses were estimated by gel permeation chromatography on a Superose 6 10/30 column.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate molecular mass using gel permeation chromatography

methods.method_name: Isolation and Analysis of Core Proteins
methods.description: Digestion of radiolabeled SLRPs with chondroitinase ABC and analysis of core proteins using chromatography and SDS-PAGE.

steps.description: The 125I-labeled SLRPs were digested with chondroitinase ABC for 24 h at 37°C.
steps.searchable_summary: digest radiolabeled SLRPs with chondroitinase ABC

steps.description: The 125I-labeled core proteins were applied to a Superose 6 column or onto an SDS-polyacrylamide gel to determine molecular mass.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze core proteins using chromatography and SDS-PAGE

methods.method_name: Isolation and Analysis of Glycosaminoglycan Chains
methods.description: Precipitation and analysis of glycosaminoglycan chains from proteoglycans using ethanol precipitation and chromatography.

steps.description: Proteoglycans were precipitated with ethanol and collected by centrifugation.
steps.searchable_summary: precipitate proteoglycans with ethanol

steps.description: The precipitated material was resuspended in NaOH containing NaB3H4 and allowed to react for 24 h at 45°C.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend and react precipitated material with NaOH and NaB3H4

steps.description: The samples were neutralized with acetic acid, dried, and washed with methanol.
steps.searchable_summary: neutralize and wash samples with acetic acid and methanol

steps.description: The samples were resuspended in phosphate-buffered saline and applied to a DEAE-Sepharose column for GAG elution.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend and apply samples to DEAE-Sepharose column

steps.description: Fractions were analyzed for radioactivity and by gel permeation chromatography for molecular size estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fractions for radioactivity and molecular size
